title: anthoxanthum aristatum subsp. puelii
intro: Anthoxanthum aristatum subsp. puelii, also known as sweet grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the grass family Poaceae. Traditionally used in folk medicine, sweet grass is known for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Its medicinal properties have been used to aid in digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and soothe respiratory issues, offering various health benefits when consumed in moderation. Sweet grass is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be smoked or dried for later use.
